title: Label-contribution declaration — schema companion
description: JSON Schema for a capability's `label-contributions.yaml` (shipped at `.pkit/capabilities/<cap>/label-contributions.yaml`). Per project-management:DEC-042, a capability declares the custom labels it needs, each pairing a stable `id` with a `default_name`, `color`, and `description`; pm provisions them at bootstrap and warns at pre-check when one is missing, collecting them by walking the manifest-registered capabilities. The matching declaration in `schemas/` documents the format and is paired with this validator.

schema_version.description: The declaration-format version. pm reads version 1; a contributor pinned to a different version has its declaration skipped-and-warned at collect time (the read-time compatibility check, DEC-042 D2).

labels.description: List of label requirements. An empty list is valid (a capability that ships the file but declares no label).

id.description: Stable handle for the contribution. A consumer resolves the label's name through `resolve_contributed_label(id)` rather than hard-coding the text (DEC-042 D5), so a future adopter override can relocate the name behind the seam. Kebab-case.

default_name.description: The label's default GitHub name (what bootstrap creates and pre-check checks for). An adopter override (v2) can relocate it; v1 uses this value.

color.description: The label's GitHub colour — six hexadecimal digits, no leading `#`.
